title: Chip War
author: Chris Miller
comment: A definitive look at why semiconductor geography is the new geopolitics. Essential reading for understanding the global high-tech supply chain and strategic competition.

title: The Innovator's Dilemma
author: Clayton M. Christensen
comment: Foundational reading on how disruptive technologies can catch established leaders off guard. A classic that remains highly relevant for today's rapid industrial shifts.

title: AI Superpowers
author: Kai-Fu Lee
comment: Deep insight into the US-China AI race and its global implications. Lee provides a unique perspective on how implementation, not just invention, will drive the next wave of AI growth.

title: The China Questions
author: Jennifer Rudolph & Michael Szonyi
comment: Critical perspectives on the issues that will shape China's future, from governance and economics to technology and social change.

title: Wenn China angreift
author: Wolfgang Hirn
comment: A provocative scenario-based analysis of potential conflict and its global economic consequences. Hirn explores the fragile balance of power and what it means for the West.

title: Prediction Machines
author: Agrawal, Gans, and Goldfarb
comment: A practical framework for understanding the economics of artificial intelligence. It reframes AI as a drop in the cost of prediction, which has profound implications for strategy.

title: Human + Machine
author: Paul R. Daugherty & H. James Wilson
comment: Reimagining work in the age of AI and industrial transformation. It explores the 'missing middle' where humans and machines collaborate to achieve superior results.
